title: Ines and the Mixed-Up Day
synopsis: When little Ines feels confused by all her different emotions in one day, her loving family helps her understand that it's okay to feel many things at once.

pages.text: Ines woke up feeling happy. The sun was bright and birds were singing. But then she remembered something that made her feel sad.

illustration.description: A young Latina girl with curly dark hair sits up in bed, stretching happily with sunshine streaming through colorful curtains. Her expression shows mixed emotions - a smile that's turning uncertain. The bedroom has warm yellow walls and cheerful decorations.
illustration.alt: Ines waking up in her sunny bedroom with mixed emotions

pages.text: Today was the day her best friend Maria was moving away. Ines felt happy about the sunny day but sad about saying goodbye. She didn't understand why she felt both things at once.

illustration.description: Ines sits on her bed looking confused, holding a photo of herself with an Asian girl (Maria) both smiling. Her face shows puzzlement with furrowed brows. The room has scattered toys and a packed moving box visible through the window next door.
illustration.alt: Ines looking confused while holding a photo of her and Maria

pages.text: At breakfast, Mama noticed Ines was quiet. 'What's wrong, mi amor?' Mama asked. Ines felt scared to talk about her mixed-up feelings.

illustration.description: A warm kitchen scene with Ines sitting at a colorful table looking down at her cereal, while her mother (a woman with long dark hair in a yellow apron) leans down with a concerned, caring expression. The kitchen has bright red and blue accents with morning light.
illustration.alt: Ines looking worried at breakfast while her caring mother asks what's wrong

pages.text: Ines took a deep breath and felt brave. 'I'm happy and sad at the same time,' she said. 'Is that okay?' Mama smiled warmly and hugged her tight.

illustration.description: Mama hugs Ines close in the bright kitchen, both with peaceful, loving expressions. Papa (a tall man with glasses and a beard) stands nearby smiling supportively. The scene feels warm and safe with golden morning light and family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: A loving family hug in the kitchen as Ines shares her feelings

pages.text: 'Having many feelings is normal,' Papa explained. 'You can feel happy about sunshine and sad about Maria leaving. Both feelings are important and real.'

illustration.description: Papa kneels down to Ines's level in the living room, using gentle hand gestures to explain. Ines listens with growing understanding, her face brightening. The room has a cozy couch with colorful pillows and family photos showing diverse friends and relatives.
illustration.alt: Papa explaining feelings to Ines in their cozy living room

pages.text: Ines felt much better understanding her feelings. She felt brave enough to say goodbye to Maria and happy knowing they would always be friends. Sometimes feeling many things at once is perfectly okay.

illustration.description: Ines and Maria hug goodbye outside, both smiling through happy tears. Other diverse neighborhood children wave in the background. The scene shows bright sunshine with moving trucks, capturing both the sadness of goodbye and the joy of friendship. Both girls look peaceful and understanding.
illustration.alt: Ines and Maria sharing a understanding goodbye hug

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Ines feel when she woke up?
questions.explanation: Ines felt happy when she woke up because of the sunny day, but then felt sad when she remembered Maria was moving away.

questions.question: What made Ines feel scared?
questions.explanation: Ines felt scared to talk about her mixed-up feelings, but she was brave and told Mama anyway.

questions.question: What did Papa teach Ines?
questions.explanation: Papa explained that having many feelings at the same time is normal and that both happy and sad feelings are important.

questions.question: How did Ines feel at the end?
questions.explanation: Ines felt much better after understanding her feelings and knowing it was okay to feel many things at once.

pages.title: Complete Ines's Story
pages.instruction: Fill in the missing words to complete the story about Ines and her feelings!
pages.text: Ines woke up feeling _____ because the sun was bright. But then she felt _____ because her friend was moving away. When she told Mama about her feelings, she felt _____. Papa explained that having many feelings is _____.
